chunk_summary: Adolescents require tailored macronutrient strategies to support growth, energy needs, and prevent risks like obesity, erratic eating, and ultraprocessed food consumption. Focus on balanced meals, protein pairing, and avoiding sugary beverages or energy drinks.
learning_objectives: Understand macronutrient priorities for adolescents aged 10-18 years, including protein pairing and meal regularity.
Identify dietary pitfalls such as sugary beverages, energy drinks, and unregulated protein powders.
Recognize red flags like binge-purge behavior, rapid weight loss, or symptoms of T2DM in adolescents.
Learn family-based strategies and follow-up approaches for adolescent nutrition management.
key_facts: Adolescents experience rapid linear growth and higher energy expenditure, requiring tailored nutrition strategies.
BMI-for-age charts and diet-beverage-activity screens are recommended for initial assessment (IAP guidelines).
Sugary beverages bypass fullness signals; removing them is a priority for weight management.
Sports drinks are only appropriate for prolonged high-intensity activity, not routine school days.
Red flags include amenorrhea, syncope, depression, binge-purge behavior, and suspected NAFLD or T2DM symptoms.
reference_quote: Liquid calories bypass fullness signals. First we remove sugar-sweetened beverages and energy drinks, then adjust snack quality; we do not crash diet or cut protein.
document_key_topics: Red flags for eating disorders and metabolic conditions
chunk_heading: MACRONUTRIENT NUTRITION ACROSS CHILDHOOD;AGE-SPECIFIC MACRONUTRIENT DECISIONS IN ADOLESCENTS TEN TO EIGHTEEN YEARS;
